name: Ghidra
description: NSA's open-source software reverse engineering suite. Disassembly, decompilation, graphing, and scripting for compiled code analysis.

name: radare2
description: UNIX-like reverse engineering framework and command-line toolset. Disassembly, debugging, and binary analysis for many architectures.

name: Cutter
description: Free reverse engineering platform powered by Rizin. GUI with graph views, decompiler integration, and extensible plugin system.

name: Binary Ninja
description: Interactive disassembler, decompiler, and binary analysis platform. C++, Python, and Rust APIs for automation.

name: IDA Free
description: Free version of the industry-standard Interactive DisAssembler. Supports x86/x64 disassembly and decompilation for non-commercial use.

name: x64dbg
description: Open-source x64/x32 debugger for Windows. Built for reverse engineering and malware analysis with a comprehensive plugin system.

name: Frida
description: Dynamic instrumentation toolkit for developers and security researchers. Hook functions and trace code in running processes without source.

name: Hopper
description: Disassembler and decompiler for macOS and Linux. Decompiles to pseudo-C, supports Mach-O, ELF, and PE formats.

name: DOSBox-X
description: Cross-platform DOS emulator with extended features. Also emulates Windows 3.x and 9x environments beyond standard DOS gaming.

name: Wine
description: Compatibility layer that translates Windows API calls to POSIX. Run Windows applications on Linux and macOS without a VM.

name: RetroArch
description: Frontend for the libretro emulation API. Run classic games across dozens of emulator cores with shaders, netplay, and rewinding.

name: Dolphin Emulator
description: GameCube and Wii emulator. Play games in HD with enhancements like higher resolution, netplay, and controller customization.

name: RPCS3
description: Open-source PlayStation 3 emulator and debugger. Nearly 70% of PS3 games playable, with active development.

name: PCSX2
description: Open-source PlayStation 2 emulator. 99% of PS2 games playable with upscaling, anti-aliasing, and texture filtering.

name: Cemu
description: Open-source Wii U emulator. Runs the majority of Wii U games smoothly with GamePad and Pro Controller emulation.

name: mGBA
description: Accurate Game Boy Advance emulator. Also supports Game Boy and Game Boy Color with link cable emulation and rewinding.

name: DeSmuME
description: Open-source Nintendo DS emulator. Tools for speedrunners, TAS, and casual play with save states and cheat support.

name: ROS 2
description: Robot Operating System. Libraries and tools for building robot applications, from drivers to state-of-the-art algorithms.

name: Gazebo
description: Open-source robotics simulator with high-fidelity physics, rendering, and sensor models. Integrates with ROS 2.

name: MoveIt
description: Motion planning framework for robotic arms. Used on 150+ robots for manipulation, path planning, and collision avoidance.

name: ArduPilot
description: Open-source autopilot for drones, planes, rovers, and submarines. Installed on over 1 million vehicles worldwide.

name: PX4
description: Professional open-source autopilot for drones and autonomous vehicles. Part of the Linux Foundation's Dronecode project.

name: Open3D
description: Modern library for 3D data processing. Point clouds, meshes, and RGBD images with optimized C++ backend and Python frontend.

name: PyBullet
description: Python module for physics simulation, robotics, and reinforcement learning. Based on the Bullet Physics SDK.

name: OpenOCD
description: Open on-chip debugger providing JTAG/SWD access from GDB. Supports ARM, MIPS, and RISC-V with flash programming.

name: KiCad
description: Free schematic capture and PCB design suite. Professional-grade EDA with 3D viewer, simulation, and Gerber output.
